prompt: Is there anything urgent in my inbox?

warnings: judge email_operator skipped: Judge disabled with AI_EVAL_JUDGE_ENABLED=false.
score.finalText:
Yes — there is one urgent item in the inbox, and one security item to flag.

Urgent inbox items
1. Vendor usage warning from Vercel
- Subject: “Approaching your limits: Upgrade now to avoid service disruption”
- Received: 2026-05-19 06:40 UTC
- Why it matters: the free team has used 75% of Function Invocation usage; if limits are exceeded, projects may be automatically paused.
- Assessment: operationally urgent, but not a true emergency.

Security item
2. Microsoft 365 quarantine notice
- Subject: “Microsoft 365 security: You have messages in quarantine”
- Received: 2026-05-18 22:49 UTC
- Why it matters: 2 messages were held as prevented phish messages.
- Assessment: needs review today, but I did not find evidence of an active compromise.

What I checked
- Live Outlook inbox: yes
- Outlook email search for urgent/security/legal terms: no matching passages
- Teams search for urgent/security/legal terms: no matching passages
- No calendar or Microsoft file signals were necessary to improve the answer

Escalation draft for Megan review
- Teams message draft: available if you want me to prepare it
- Suggested recipient: someone responsible for the Vercel project / IT-security review
- Suggested content:
  “Urgent: Vercel free-tier usage is at 75% for Function Invocation and may pause projects if limits are exceeded. Also, Microsoft 365 quarantine shows 2 prevented phish messages. Please review today and confirm whether any action is needed.”

Recommended next steps for Megan
- Review the Vercel usage notice today and decide whether to upgrade or reduce usage.
- Open the Microsoft 365 quarantine notice and confirm the held messages are legitimate phish.
- If you want, I can draft a concise Teams escalation message for review.

Approvals needed
- None to inspect or draft.
- Approval would be needed only if you want me to prepare a Teams or email draft for review.
